positions.claim: Scarcity and shared cultural moments are the primary drivers of media asset value in the internet age, not content quality or production values
implicitAssumptions: Abundance of content has permanently destroyed the ability to monetize quality alone
Synchronous consumption (everyone watching at the same time) creates more economic value than asynchronous consumption regardless of total audience size
Network effects from shared viewing experiences compound faster than improvements in content production technology

positions.claim: AI will cause rapid, chaotic economic disruption including 10%+ unemployment and 40% S&P 500 drawdowns within a measurable timeframe
implicitAssumptions: AI capabilities will improve faster than labor markets can retrain or reallocate workers
Professional services work (tax planning, wealth management) has lower defensibility against automation than physical or creative work
Market pricing mechanisms will anticipate AI displacement before it fully materializes in employment data

positions.claim: External metrics from technology platforms (followers, likes, portfolio performance, step counts) have become the primary drivers of daily emotional states and decision-making for most people
implicitAssumptions: Quantified feedback loops are more psychologically powerful than qualitative self-assessment
The immediacy and frequency of metric updates matters more than the underlying reality they measure
People lack effective cognitive defenses against gamified metric systems even when aware of their manipulative design

positions.claim: Compounding returns over multi-decade timeframes are the only reliable path to exceptional investment performance, not timing or stock picking
implicitAssumptions: The psychological difficulty of maintaining discipline over 60 years is a greater barrier than analytical skill in achieving market-beating returns
Market inefficiencies that enable outperformance persist across decades rather than being arbitraged away
The difference between 19.9% and 10.4% annual returns compounds to such extreme wealth that no other investment strategy justifies consideration

positions.claim: AI-generated content will overwhelm and degrade platform quality faster than platforms can develop effective filtering mechanisms
implicitAssumptions: The cost of generating AI content decreases faster than the cost of detecting and filtering it
False positive rates in AI detection are economically more damaging to platforms than false negatives
User-generated content platforms have structural incentives that prevent them from aggressively filtering AI content even when technically capable

positions.claim: Dream-adjacent work (jobs close to but not exactly your ambition) is more dangerous to long-term achievement than completely unrelated work
implicitAssumptions: Psychological satisfaction from proximity to a dream provides enough dopamine to suppress the discomfort that drives action
The social identity benefits of dream-adjacent work (telling people you work at a video store when you want to make films) create false progress signals
Complete disconnection from a dream maintains higher psychological tension than partial connection

positions.claim: Aggressive marketing spend (12% of revenue) in performance channels like TikTok and Instagram is the primary driver of hypergrowth in consumer product categories
implicitAssumptions: Influencer-driven product discovery has higher conversion rates than traditional advertising despite appearing less targeted
Consumer product categories can support 24x revenue growth primarily through marketing rather than product innovation
Instagram's hyper-targeting algorithms are effective enough to justify premium ad spend even for commodity-like products

positions.claim: Short-form video formatting (30-second clips with sensory overlays) is the only viable distribution mechanism for classic cultural content to reach younger audiences
implicitAssumptions: Gen-Z attention patterns are fundamentally different from previous generations rather than just expressing age-typical preferences
Cultural literacy can be effectively transmitted through decontextualized clips rather than requiring full narrative experiences
The format of content delivery matters more than the intrinsic quality or cultural significance of the content itself

positions.claim: Streaming TV shows systematically decline in quality after strong starts, making it rational to refuse watching new shows regardless of initial reviews
implicitAssumptions: Streaming business models create structural incentives for shows to prioritize subscriber acquisition over narrative coherence
The time cost of watching a show that deteriorates is higher than the opportunity cost of missing a rare show that maintains quality
Pattern recognition from past viewing experiences is a more reliable quality signal than critical reviews or social recommendations

positions.claim: Market reactions to AI capability announcements are driven more by investor anxiety about professional services displacement than by rational assessment of economic impact
implicitAssumptions: Professional services workers are overrepresented in investment decision-making relative to their share of the economy
Markets price AI risk through sector rotation rather than through systematic repricing of all labor-exposed equities
The speed of market reactions to AI news exceeds the speed at which AI capabilities actually deploy in enterprise settings

positions.claim: AI systems will fail at customer-facing tasks requiring contextual judgment (like drive-thru orders) before they fail at professional knowledge work
implicitAssumptions: Edge cases and adversarial inputs are more common in consumer-facing applications than in professional settings
The reputational cost of visible AI failures in consumer settings exceeds the cost savings from automation
Professional knowledge work has more structured inputs that are easier for AI to handle than unstructured consumer interactions

positions.claim: Exposure to creator content (60-second motivational clips, creative process documentaries) directly triggers behavioral urges that shape daily actions
implicitAssumptions: Parasocial relationships with creators generate stronger behavioral modeling than in-person relationships
The emotional intensity of short-form content creates more durable behavioral changes than long-form content despite less information transfer
Seeing evidence of others' extreme discipline or creativity triggers imitation more effectively than abstract advice or instruction
